query_basis: Programs table rows with verified state program evidence.
Waiver-like program rows or fallback to verified program rows when the state stores waiver entry in the general spine.
Verified early-intervention or Part C agency rows.
Verified regional education or district pages with special-education routing.

samples.sample_name: Idaho State Department of Education Special Education

query_basis: Reviewed the residual Camas and Clark official district leaves again, plus the one district-linked Camas document, the district-hosted Clark parent-notification attachments, and the district-hosted Clark Child Find flyers, while preserving Jefferson, Oneida, Fremont, and Shoshone as positive local routing recoveries.
blocker_evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 one more bounded live Idaho district pass on the exact residual Camas and Clark hosts. Camas root and `Contact Information` are still public HTTP 200 pages, but `https://www.camascountyschools.org/sitemap.xml` now returns HTTP 404. A fresh same-host href scan across the reviewed Camas pages bottoms out at `home`, `contact-information`, and `all-resources`; the rendered `all-resources` page now exposes same-host `Food Services`, `Federal Programs`, and `Advanced Opportunities` leaves, but those leaves still stay the wrong role. The live `Federal Programs` page only preserves Title I and Title IX compliance/training material plus coordinator contact, and the live `Advanced Opportunities` page only preserves accelerated-course guidance. Neither page exposes district-owned special-education, student-services, 504, Child Find, or procedural-safeguards routing. The district-linked Google Doc still exports only a board-roster lane rather than special-education routing. Clark root and `Parent Resources` still return HTTP 200, `https://www.clarkcountyschools161.org/sitemap.xml` now returns HTTP 404, and the older short aliases `/contact-us`, `/title-ix`, and `/parent-notification-of-general-education-instruction` now return HTTP 404. Their current replacement exact leaves remain live instead: `/about-us/contact-us-ccsd`, `/administration/title-ix`, and `/about-us/parent-notification-of-general-education-instruction` all return HTTP 200 on the same district host. A fresh same-host anchor-text scan across the live Clark `Parent Resources` page still bottoms out in wrong-role or too-thin lanes: the live replacement `Title IX` and `Parent Notification of General Education Instruction` leaves, food-service pages, a literacy plan, and the same district-hosted `Idaho Child Find` English and Spanish flyer PDFs, but no district-owned special-education, special-services, student-services, 504, or procedural-safeguards leaf with local contact evidence. Idaho therefore remains blocked because the final district-owned surfaces are still public but remain the wrong role or too thin for local special-education routing.

samples.evidence_snippet: The official School Districts page preserves 116 exact outbound district website links and district names including some county-bearing names such as Blaine County District #61 and Boundary County District #101, but it does not expose a county field or district special-education contacts.

The public WordPress page JSON mirrors the district names and outbound website links from the rendered directory page, confirming the official link inventory without adding county-routing or special-education fields.

A live DB reconciliation still shows all 44 Idaho county education rows pointing at statewide SDE fallbacks rather than reviewed district-owned leaves: 42 rows use /sped/ and 2 rows use the generic SDE root.
The district-owned Contact Information page only preserves Camas district address and phone; it does not expose special-education, student-services, 504, Child Find, or procedural-safeguards routing.

samples.sample_name: Camas linked Google Doc board roster
samples.evidence_snippet: The linked document exports only board-of-trustees zones and trustee names, not special-education, student-services, 504, Child Find, or procedural-safeguards routing.
The district-owned Contact Us page lists district office staff, superintendent, address, and phone, but no special-education or student-services routing.
The district-owned Title IX page only links Title IX policy and repeats generic accessibility language; it does not expose special-education, 504, or student-services routing.

samples.sample_name: Clark Parent Notification of General Education Instruction leaf
samples.evidence_snippet: The district-owned page title is `Parent Notification of General Education Instruction`; it preserves general-education intervention notice routing, not special-education, student-services, 504, or procedural-safeguards routing.

The district-owned Parent Resources page links Idaho Child Find English and Spanish PDFs, but the page itself still preserves no district special-education contact, student-services contact, or local routing language.

The district-hosted attachments sit behind the Parent Notification of General Education Instruction page and remain part of the same general-education intervention notice lane, not a local special-education or student-services routing contract.

The district-hosted Child Find flyer PDFs linked from Parent Resources are live, but bounded inspection only confirms image-only flyer artifacts with no extractable Clark-, Dubois-, phone-, screening-, or district-specific special-education routing evidence.

query_basis: Verified programs with VR or Pre-ETS context.
Reviewed first-party Disability Rights Idaho evidence on disk explicitly preserves Idaho Protection and Advocacy designation text plus statewide help-routing.

samples.evidence_snippet: As the Protection and Advocacy agency for Idaho, DRI serves the whole state. We offer information and referrals to residents with disabilities.

query_basis: Reviewed first-party Idaho Parents Unlimited About and Connect With Us pages now preserve explicit PTI designation plus statewide contact routing.

samples.evidence_snippet: Founded in 1985, Idaho Parents Unlimited, Inc. (IPUL) is a statewide organization which houses the Idaho Parent Training and Information Center, the Family to Family Health Information Center, Idaho Family Voices and IPUL Arts.
Idaho Parents Unlimited, Inc. 4619 W Emerald St, Ste. E Boise, ID 83706 Phone: (208) 342-5884 Email: parents@ipulidaho.org.

query_basis: Reviewed first-party Disability Rights Idaho evidence on disk explicitly preserves free legal services, attorneys, and statewide disability-rights representation routing.

samples.evidence_snippet: We also offer free legal services. DRI’s attorneys can provide representation to individuals with disabilities in negotiations, mediations, or by filing complaints or a lawsuit.

query_basis: Verified ABLE program pages from the program spine.
Reviewed live official Idaho DHW office root plus one exact office leaf, then reconciled those live sources against the deterministic county-local office packet.
blocker_evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 one more bounded live Idaho DHW office-contract pass on the exact official office root, one exact office leaf, the paginated office inventory, and the now-public sitemap. `https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/offices` remains a live `Find a Service Location` root, `https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/dhw/caldwell-office` remains a live exact office leaf, the live paginated office inventory at `/offices?page=0`, `/offices?page=1`, and `/offices?page=2` still returns HTTP 200, and `https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/sitemap.xml` now returns HTTP 200. But those reviewed public surfaces still preserve office inventory only, not county assignment: the paginated office pages expose office names such as `Grangeville Office - Camas Resource Center` yet still expose no `county served`, `counties served`, `serves`, or `service area` field at all, and the public sitemap only enumerates exact office leaves such as Coeur d’Alene, Caldwell, Payette, Boise Westgate, Mountain Home, Burley, Twin Falls, Blackfoot, Pocatello, Idaho Falls, Rexburg, Salmon, and Kellogg. The county-named URLs that do appear on the same host are still unrelated food-bank or pantry pages like `adams-county-mobile-pantry-council`, `camas-county-mobile-pantry-fairfield`, and `clark-county-mobile-pantry-dubois`. The live DHW stack still preserves no county-served fields, county-to-office table, service-area contract, or county assignment export for disability-routing offices. Idaho county-local routing therefore remains an explicit split between the existing safe exact-office replacements and the legacy counties that still lack a public county contract.

samples.evidence_snippet: The live DHW office root is titled Find a Service Location and still preserves exact city office cards like Caldwell Office, but it still exposes no public county terms or county-served fields in reviewable HTML.
Caldwell Office is still a live exact DHW office leaf and remains the safe county-office replacement inside the 17 clean exact replacements set.
The deterministic Idaho county-local packet still materializes 17 clean exact county-to-office replacements plus an explicit Canyon split and 27 unresolved legacy counties.

samples.sample_name: Nampa mention resolves only to SWITC
samples.evidence_snippet: The only public Nampa mention still resolves to Southwest Idaho Treatment Center (SWITC), not to a county office or benefits office leaf.

samples.sample_name: Idaho DHW public sitemap office inventory
samples.evidence_snippet: The live DHW sitemap now returns HTTP 200 and enumerates exact office leaves like Caldwell, Payette, Boise Westgate, Burley, Twin Falls, Pocatello, Idaho Falls, Rexburg, Salmon, and Kellogg, but the county-named URLs on the same host are unrelated food-bank or pantry pages rather than county-to-office disability-routing contracts.
